title: Phillips Edison & Company Declares Monthly Dividend Distributions; Announces Results of Annual Meeting of Stockholders
author: Globe Newswire
description: Phillips Edison & Company (PECO), a major owner and operator of grocery-anchored neighborhood shopping centers, declared monthly dividend distributions of $0.1083 per share payable in July and August 2026. The company held its annual stockholder meeting where all directors were re-elected and shareholders approved executive compensation and the appointment of Deloitte & Touche LLP as the independent auditor for 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared consistent monthly dividend distributions and successfully held its annual meeting with full board re-election and shareholder approvals, indicating stable operations and investor confidence. The maintenance of regular dividend payments demonstrates financial health and commitment to shareholders.

title: What to Know About One Fund's $7 Million Sale of a Grocery REIT Up 9% This Past Year
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Sophron Capital Management sold 202,175 shares of Phillips Edison & Company (PECO) for approximately $7.02 million in Q4, reducing its stake from 6.1% to 4.2% of AUM. The sale appears to be a portfolio rebalancing move rather than a loss of confidence, as PECO remains fundamentally strong with 97% occupancy and growing net income. The fund maintains significant exposure to real estate REITs through other major holdings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company demonstrates operational strength with 97% occupancy, net income growth from $62.7M to $111.3M YoY, and stable funds from operations of $353.1M. The fund's sale appears to be portfolio rebalancing rather than a loss of confidence. Grocery-anchored centers provide durable cash flows from essential retail tenants.

title: 3 Stocks That Cut You a Check Each Month
author: Dave Kovaleski
description: The article highlights three stocks that pay monthly dividends to shareholders: Realty Income (O), a REIT with 667 consecutive months of dividend payments and 32 years of increases; Main Street Capital (MAIN), a BDC with 18 years of annual dividend increases; and Phillips Edison (PECO), a grocery-anchored retail REIT with four consecutive years of dividend raises. These companies offer consistent income through monthly distributions, with dividend yields ranging from 3.30% to 6.96%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company has demonstrated exceptional consistency with 667 consecutive months of dividend payments, 32 years of consecutive dividend increases, high occupancy rate of 98.7%, and solid 10-year annualized returns of 6.4% with dividend reinvestment.

Strong track record with 18 consecutive years of annual dividend increases since inception, high dividend yield of 6.96%, and impressive 10-year annualized returns of 16.3% with dividend reinvestment, which exceeds S&P 500 performance.

Consistent dividend growth with four consecutive years of increases since going public, strong occupancy rate of 97.3%, solid 7% annualized returns (11% with dividend reinvestment), and strategic focus on stable grocery-anchored neighborhood shopping centers.

title: Phillips Edison & Company Declares Monthly Dividends
author: Globe Newswire
description: Phillips Edison & Company (NASDAQ: PECO) announced monthly dividend distributions of $0.1083 per share payable on April 1, May 1, and June 2, 2026. The company, one of the nation's largest owners and operators of grocery-anchored neighborhood shopping centers, manages 324 shopping centers across 31 states with major grocery anchors including Kroger, Publix, and Albertsons.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared consistent monthly dividend distributions, demonstrating financial stability and commitment to shareholder returns. The regular dividend payments indicate strong cash flow generation from its portfolio of 324 well-occupied shopping centers anchored by major grocery retailers.

title: Phillips Edison & Company Announces Tax Reporting Information for 2025 Distributions
author: Globe Newswire
description: Phillips Edison & Company (NASDAQ: PECO), a major owner and operator of grocery-anchored neighborhood shopping centers, released its tax reporting information for 2025 distributions to common stock holders. The company provided detailed per-share breakdowns of ordinary dividends, capital gains, and other distribution components across quarterly payments throughout 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article is a routine tax reporting disclosure with no material business developments, earnings surprises, or strategic announcements. It provides standard administrative information about dividend composition for tax purposes, which is neither positive nor negative for investors.
